div.p: Sequential memory access pattern with cache-efficient heap-based top-k selection
Color Legend: 🟢 Cache-efficient | 🔴 Cache-inefficient | 🔵 Logic | 🟡 Hardware
[v0_dim0, v0_dim1, ..., v0_dim767] [v1_dim0, v1_dim1, ..., v1_dim767] [v2_dim0, v2_dim1, ..., v2_dim767] ... [vN_dim0, vN_dim1, ..., vN_dim767]
Vector i starts at: offset = i × padded_dim

text.tspan: // vector points to aligned memory region
// No pointer chasing - pure sequential access
// vector points to aligned memory region
// No pointer chasing - pure sequential access
// O(log k) per update, NOT O(log N)
// Max-heap keeps worst candidate at root
// O(log k) per update, NOT O(log N)
// Max-heap keeps worst candidate at root

div.p: Sequential Scan: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 50+ GB/s Random Access: ╺ <1 GB/s
Key Insight: Brute-force is production-ready for datasets < 100K vectors. Latency remains sub-50ms on modern hardware.
